205.reason: [Same issue as with '&': a single '|' should trigger an error, but encoding the error as a Token of type ERROR_A with content=line number violates the intended separation between token stream and error reporting. The output class then tries to write this "token" to lexer.txt instead of writing to error.txt, causing incorrect output format. According to the problem, errors must be written to error.txt in the format "line_number error_code", not embedded in the token list.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Same issue as with '&': a single '|' should trigger an error, but encoding the error as a Token of type ERROR_A with content=line number violates the intended separation between token stream and error reporting. The output class then tries to write this "token" to lexer.txt instead of writing to error.txt, causing incorrect output format. According to the problem, errors must be written to error.txt in the format "line_number error_code", not embedded in the token list.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Same issue: if '|' is the last character in the line, after pos++, pos >= length, so it breaks without reporting the error. Thus, trailing single '|' is not detected as error type 'a'.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [When encountering a single '&' or '|', the lexer adds an ERROR_A token but continues processing; however, according to the problem, the program should stop and output only the error. But more critically, the error handling logic in output.java assumes only one error token exists, yet the lexer may add multiple tokens including valid ones before/after the error. However, the primary lexical error detection here is correct. The bigger issue is in Token.java's enum typo.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 1.0 , reason:  [Same issue as with '&': a single '|' triggers ERROR_A, but the token is added to the general token list with line number as content, which breaks the expected separation between valid tokens and errors. The lexical analyzer should not add error tokens to the same list used for valid output; instead, it should signal an error and let the output module write to error.txt directly or via a separate error list. This design flaw causes incorrect output handling.]

59.reason: [Typo in "COSNTTK" should be "CONSTTK", which will cause keyword "const" to be misclassified as IDENFR instead of CONSTTK]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Typo in "COSNTTK" should be "CONSTTK", which will cause keyword "const" to be misclassified as IDENFR instead of CONSTTK]

395.reason: [This converts lowercase keywords to uppercase + "TK", e.g., "main" becomes "MAINTK", which matches expected output. However, because of the typo "COSNTTK" in Token.java, "const" is stored as KEYWORD but when output tries to map it, it uses the content "const", converts to "CONSTTK", but the enum doesn't have CONSTTK—it has COSNTTK—so during token creation, "const" was not recognized as a keyword at all and was likely classified as IDENFR. Thus, this output logic never sees "const" as KEYWORD, so it won't output CONSTTK.]

Thus, this output logic never sees "const" as KEYWORD, so it won't output CONSTTK.]

401.reason: [The logic assumes that if any ERROR_A token exists, sign==1, and outputs to lexer.txt; but the problem requires writing errors to error.txt, not lexer.txt. Additionally, the program never creates error.txt.]
